ud3.9:1.2: on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī at Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
ud3.9:1.3:
Now at that time a large number of monks, after the meal, on returning from their alms round,
were sitting gathered together at a pavilion when this discussion arose:
ud3.9:1.6: Which is the supreme among crafts?”
ud3.9:2.1: With regard to that, some said,
ud3.9:2.2: “The elephant-craft is the supreme craft among crafts.”
ud3.9:2.4: “The horse-craft is the supreme craft among crafts” …
ud3.9:2.24: “Geomancy is the supreme craft among crafts.”
ud3.9:2.25: And this discussion came to no conclusion.
ud3.9:3.1:
Then the Blessed One, emerging from his seclusion in the late afternoon,
went to the pavilion and, on arrival, sat down on a seat laid out.
ud3.9:3.2: As he was sitting there, he addressed the monks:
ud3.9:3.3:
“For what topic are you sitting together here?
And what was the discussion that came to no conclusion?”
ud3.9:4.1:
“Just now, lord, after the meal, on returning from our alms round,
we were sitting gathered together here at the pavilion when this discussion arose:
[They repeat what had been said.]”
ud3.9:6.1:
“It isn’t proper, monks, that sons of good families,
on having gone forth out of faith from home to the homeless life, should talk on such a topic.
ud3.9:6.2: When you have gathered you have two duties:
ud3.9:7.1: Then, on realizing the significance of that, the Blessed One on that occasion exclaimed:
ud3.9:8.1: Supporting himself without a craft– light, desiring the goal–
ud3.9:8.3: living in no home, unpossessive, free from longing,
ud3.9:8.4:
having slain Māra, going alone :
a monk.
